note: Expand Collapse Formatter provides a single core @FieldFormatter plugin (id expand_collapse_formatter) for long-text fields; it has no settings form of its own (configure=null). Its entire persistent state is the formatter component in an entity_view_display config entity (settings key field.formatter.settings.expand_collapse_formatter). Easy cases probe documented facts (formatter id, field types, settings/defaults). Medium/hard cases are grounded in the real view-display config entity core.entity_view_display.node.article.default: introspection reads the formatter type + settings the setup script wrote onto the Article body; execution switches the Article body to this formatter with specific settings and verifies the live component. The reset restores the body to its core text_default formatter.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want a long text field to show trimmed with a JavaScript 'Show more / Show less' toggle. Which contrib module provides this, and what is the machine id of the field formatter I select on Manage display?
evals.expected_output: Use the Expand Collapse Formatter module; select the formatter whose id is expand_collapse_formatter (label 'Expand collapse formatter') on the field's Manage display.

evals.prompt: Which field types can the Expand collapse formatter (expand_collapse_formatter) be applied to? Can I use it on a plain single-line string field or an integer field?
evals.expected_output: It supports the long/multi-line text field types only: text_long, string_long, text_with_summary and text_long_with_summary. It cannot be used on a single-line string field or an integer field.

evals.prompt: In the Expand collapse formatter, which setting controls how much text is shown before it is truncated, what is its default value, and what is the default initial state of the field?
evals.expected_output: The trim_length setting controls the truncation length; it defaults to 300 characters. The default_state setting defaults to 'collapsed'. The toggle link only appears when the field's text is longer than trim_length.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, which field formatter is currently configured for the Article content type's body field on its default view display, and what trim length is it set to? Inspect the live entity view display configuration to answer.
evals.expected_output: The Article body field on core.entity_view_display.node.article.default uses the expand_collapse_formatter formatter with trim_length 250 (default_state collapsed, open link text 'Read more').

evals.prompt: The Article body field on this site uses the Expand collapse formatter. What is the 'Link text (open)' the toggle will display when the field is collapsed? Inspect the live view-display configuration for the exact string.
evals.expected_output: The body component's expand_collapse_formatter settings have link_text_open = 'Read more' (and link_text_close = 'Read less') in core.entity_view_display.node.article.default.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, configure the Article content type's default view display so the body field is rendered with the Expand Collapse Formatter (formatter id expand_collapse_formatter). Set its trim length to 150 and its default state to 'expanded'. Apply the change to the live site (config entity core.entity_view_display.node.article.default).
evals.expected_output: In core.entity_view_display.node.article.default the body component has type expand_collapse_formatter with settings.trim_length = 150 and settings.default_state = 'expanded'.

evals.prompt: On my Drupal site I want the main text of my Articles to start showing in full but with a link people can click to collapse it, and only the first 150 characters should stay once collapsed. Please set up the Article body so it works that way, using the expand/collapse read-more feature.
evals.expected_output: In core.entity_view_display.node.article.default the body component uses the expand_collapse_formatter with settings.trim_length = 150 and settings.default_state = 'expanded'.
